issue.title: Convert the derelict grain mill into the node's shared fabrication hall
issue.description: The node's fabrication work has outgrown a cramped, damp workshop. The disused 1920s grain mill is the obvious large space to convert — but its interior and original milling machinery carry deep communal and historical meaning. The node must decide what to do.

context.historical: The grain mill (1924) milled the valley's grain for three generations; several elders have family ties to it. Disused since 2011.
context.ecological: Reusing the existing shell avoids the embodied carbon of a new build; a new shed would import steel and concrete.
context.resources: The mill shell is structurally sound; conversion is mostly fit-out labor. A new shed needs purchased structural materials.
context.labor: About 220 skilled labor-hours available across the building and fabrication co-ops this window.
context.dependencies: Every production co-op depends on shared fabrication capacity; the bottleneck is slowing food, repair, and tooling work.

proposals.label: Convert the grain mill into the fabrication hall
proposals.summary: Strip and fit out the mill interior as a modern shared fabrication hall; remove the original milling machinery.

proposals.label: Build a new fabrication shed elsewhere
proposals.summary: Leave the mill untouched and build a new purpose-built shed on the cleared lot.

proposals.label: Incrementally expand the current workshop
proposals.summary: Bridge-step option: patch and extend the existing cramped workshop instead of relocating.

constraints.name: ecological_budget
constraints.message: Embodied-carbon impact exceeds the node's ecological budget.

constraints.name: accessibility_floor
constraints.message: Shared fabrication access for all members must be preserved.

constraints.name: labor_budget
constraints.message: Labor + material burden exceeds the planning-window budget.

constraints.name: heritage_review_required
constraints.message: Altering a structure older than 50 years requires a heritage review before it may proceed.

intake.author: stillwater:maya
intake.text: The current workshop is cramped and damp; we keep losing tools and time.

intake.author: stillwater:sam
intake.text: The current workshop is cramped and damp; we keep losing tools and material.

intake.author: stillwater:devon
intake.text: Half our members can't even fit in the current workshop to use the shared tools.

intake.author: stillwater:beatrice
intake.text: The old grain mill is the heart of this place — my grandfather milled there. Please don't gut it.

intake.author: stillwater:tomas
intake.text: If we must expand, reuse the mill shell — building new would import a mountain of embodied carbon.
